command.text: find /testbed -type f -name "*.txt" -exec wc -w {} + | awk 'NR>1{sum+=$1;cnt++} END{print sum/cnt}'

raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need bash command: find /testbed -type f -name "*.txt" -size -? but size by words not bytes. Use wc -w. Need filter <=2000 words. Use find with -exec wc -w {} + then process. Compute mean. Use awk. Command: find /testbed -type f -name "*.txt" -exec wc -w {} + | awk 'NR>1{sum+=$1;cnt++} END{print sum/cnt}'. But wc -w outputs "count filename". So awk uses $1. That works. Need to exclude header? wc -w with multiple files prints each line. No header. So fine. Return command.

task.query: Compute the mean average of the word count of *.txt files smaller than 2000 words in the /testbed directory
task.gold: find /testbed  -name '*.txt' -print0 | xargs -0 wc -w | awk '$1 < 2000 {v += $1; c++} END {print v/c}'
